methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

private.name: Stark Defence
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

private.name: Vatn Systems

private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

data.narrative:
Rune Technologies is a Series A-stage defense and security company founded by David Tuttle and Peter Goldsborough that has raised $20M+. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 10 private companies and 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) operating in the same sector and stage.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $20M total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage advancement, implies a valuation of $40M. Peer-median analysis examined three same-stage, same-sector comparables: Stark Defence at $500M, Vatn Systems at $300M, and Ares Industries at $150M. The median across these three peers is $300M, with a range spanning $150M to $500M.

Triangulation of these two methods supports a valuation range of $34M to $345M, with a median of $170M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around whether Rune's capital efficiency and market position align more closely with earlier-stage valuation conventions (stage-progression approach at $40M) or with the heterogeneous peer universe, which spans from bootstrapped entrants to well-capitalized platforms. Resolution of this gap depends on clarity regarding Rune's revenue traction, customer concentration, and technical differentiation relative to the peer set.
